parsing_rationale: The document was analyzed by mapping physical sections to the required template fields. Technical constraints were extracted as atomic elements with unique identifiers. Relationships were established based on dependencies (e.g., Data Access depends on the approved Stack). Structural gaps were identified for missing high-level governance categories like Branching/PR workflow details.

project_info.brief_explanation: Technical constitution defining the stack, security, API standards, and quality gates for a Learning Platform API.

sections.title: I. Technology Standardization
sections.raw_content: All implementation work must use the approved stack: Python 3.12, FastAPI, PostgreSQL 16, SQLAlchemy 2.0 with async execution, Alembic for migrations, and Pydantic v2 for validation. New features must align with this stack and avoid introducing alternative frameworks or synchronous database access patterns.

sections.title: II. Authentication and Authorization
sections.raw_content: Authentication must use JWT access tokens with a 15-minute expiry and refresh tokens with a 7-day expiry. The system supports exactly two roles: student and instructor. Instructors are authorized to create, edit, and delete courses. Students are authorized to enroll in courses and submit progress.

sections.title: III. API Contract and Response Shape
sections.raw_content: The API must be RESTful, return JSON, and be versioned under /api/v1/. All responses must use a consistent envelope of the form {"data": ..., "meta": ..., "errors": []}. API changes must preserve backward compatibility unless a new version is introduced.

sections.title: IV. Data Access and Persistence
sections.raw_content: All database access must be asynchronous. SQLAlchemy sessions must never be used synchronously, and all data access must be performed through the ORM. Raw SQL is prohibited. Schema changes must be handled through Alembic migrations.

sections.title: V. Quality and Testing
sections.raw_content: Testing must use pytest with httpx AsyncClient. Business logic coverage should target 80% or higher. Database tests must use a real PostgreSQL test instance and must not mock the database layer.

sections.title: Additional Constraints
sections.raw_content:
- Transactional email delivery must use the Resend Python SDK.
- The Resend API key must be provided through the RESEND_API_KEY environment variable.
- A welcome email must be sent to students upon registration.
- Security-sensitive behavior must be validated through integration-style tests wherever practical.

sections.title: Development Workflow
sections.raw_content:
- Features must be implemented and validated through tests before being considered complete.
- Any schema or contract change must include the corresponding migration and API documentation updates.
- Changes that affect authentication, authorization, or response envelopes require explicit review.

sections.title: Governance
sections.raw_content:
This constitution supersedes ad hoc implementation choices. Any deviation from these standards requires documented justification and review before merge.

**Version**: 1.0.0 | **Ratified**: 2026-06-27 | **Last Amended**: 2026-06-27

elements.content: Python 3.12, FastAPI, PostgreSQL 16, SQLAlchemy 2.0 (async), Alembic, Pydantic v2
JWT access tokens (15m expiry) and refresh tokens (7d expiry)
RESTful JSON API, versioned under /api/v1/, standard envelope {data, meta, errors}
elements.source_section: III. API Contract and Response Shape

elements.content: All database access must be asynchronous via ORM; Raw SQL is prohibited
Schema changes must be handled exclusively through Alembic migrations
Use pytest with httpx AsyncClient; business logic coverage >= 80%
Database tests must use a real PostgreSQL instance (no mocking)
Use Resend Python SDK with RESEND_API_KEY environment variable
Features must be validated through tests before completion
Changes to auth, authorization, or response envelopes require explicit review

structural_gaps.remediation_advice: Add a section for pending technical decisions or known trade-offs.
